steps.title: Identify the relationship the blank must express
steps.content: The passage says Puerto Rican vowel sounds connect back to Yoruba as their origin. The blank fills “can be _______ to how they are pronounced in Yoruba,” so the word must convey tracing something back to a source.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — traced: “Traced to” means identified as originating from — precisely the origin relationship the passage establishes. ✓
  • Choice B — announced: “Announced to a language” is illogical; announces means to declare publicly, not to connect linguistically. Wrong word meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — offered: “Offered to a language” does not express origin or linguistic descent. Wrong word meaning. ✗
  • Choice D — surrendered: “Surrendered to a language” implies defeat or concession, not linguistic origin. Wrong word meaning. ✗
A is correct.
